reviews.review_title: Lots of fun, but why the extra charge?
reviews.review_description:
This game is a lot of fun. Great classic on rails shooter. However having bought this game on App Lab they changed the name to Brigade instead of Vrigade. Also decided to charge more money. Not cross buy. Well with this greedy move. I’ll tell everyone I know, not to bother with it. To greedy for your own good apparently. I’m hoping it just a small problem, but so far this is the only title, that charges you once while in App Lab, and then make a small name change. To charge again. Already paid for it, where is my new version? 
     Update App Lab version was finally updated, by the end of the day of release. However; with that said. The App Lab version is probably 100% the same game. Yet actual store version still trying to charge me. Argo only game to hit marketplace from App Lab, and not just transfer over everything. Make you start all over again. Seems like developers may be having some small issues that need to be dealt with. Still say it’s a fun game, but this set up is kinda pathetic at this point.
      Yet again this game purchased through App Lab. Still says I don’t own the actual Store version. On top of that, it’s bundled in a sals bundle with Alvo, yet because it says I don’t own the store version. So the deal is jacked up in price. Only company to ever do this. I’m pretty sure it was done on purpose for a money grab. Very disappointed with the problems arising because of this issue. FIX THIS NOW.

developer_response.body: I'd like to assure you that the recent movement you're referencing is not a deliberate attempt to generate revenue. If that were the case, we would have stopped updating the AppLab version of the application. However, we assure you that we will continue to provide support for the AppLab version, and we guarantee that the AppLab version will be exactly the same as the version that is available on the Meta Store.

reviews.review_title: How a FPS VR game should be
reviews.review_description:
This game is exactly what I wanted out of a FPS VR game. For me, I wanted a FPS which didnt require alot of movement, so this is the perfect mix of shooting, ducking and crouching.

The game is very immersive and engaging and has swiftly become my favourite VR game. It is the sort of game that you can spend hours on, or just dip in for 15 minutes if thats what you are after.

bravo developers, this is a winner.

reviews.review_title: This game is so much fun
reviews.review_description: Like time crisis but in Vr

reviews.review_title: A good VR Time Crisis clone.
reviews.review_description:
A very good Shooter, almost 5 stars.
For me, I think even Rookie level is too hard.  And you cannot progress if you are lazy seat shooter. 
Make rookie level easier and it gets a 5 star.

developer_response.body: Let me clarify that playing you earn coins so you can purchase up to 5 lifes, weapons, laser sight... so you can lower the difficulty to a very, very low bar. Try it! Thank you for your review.

reviews.review_description: Best Quest VR game yet! Especially if you play in th 8x8m. It could do with more modules and more choice of guns. Almos perfect.

Bought the first one and was hooked.
Love this one too.

I feel in the first one u can reload ur gun faster so would be great if this one was tweaked to reload just as fast.
It won’t download

developer_response.body: Hello, this is not related to the game. Please, try to restart your quest and try again. If that doesn't work, please, check if you have the last quest software version.

reviews.review_description: Put Time Crisis in VR, and you have arcade heaven! The physical duck / hide for cover mechanism really gets you into the game, and I haven't even mastered rookie level enough to warrant using manual reloading. Hoping more levels will be added, and maybe a mode along the lines of duck hunt...my favorite quick fix!
i have paid for this game but i have not got it now i have been waitting all day abit concered 🙄😟

developer_response.body: Hello, the game should be in your library like any other game. Perhaps you have to update your Quest to the last version. If that doesn't work, perhaps you need to reboot the Quest.

reviews.review_description: This is easily the most fun I’ve had on my quest 2. It’s easy to pick up but so addictive I can’t put it down. Good clean arcade shooter fun with the benefits of VR. Please stop reading reviews ans treat yourself to this absolute gem.
What a game! Smooth and very immersive! Best first person shooter yet, game play is addictive, albeit quite short for a £15 game.

reviews.review_title: Excellent homage to old arcade shooters
reviews.review_description:
A very "short but sweet" kind of arcade style shooter experience. Involves a lot of ducking, and popping in & out of cover so a decently active experience while you're playing it. The currently available levels are quite a challenge, even at the lower difficulty, but since they're all short it's not a massive pain to start them over after you die. Plus you earn in-game currency with which to buy small upgrades to make the next playthrough a bit easier. 
The UI is simple but effective, and during play nothing is cluttering your screen. Graphically it's done in a lightly cell-shaded style which makes everything look very crisp & sharp, with targets standing out well against the environment. Excellent choice for visuals, I think.
Shooting is responsive and manual reloading works perfectly fine. Guns don't really "feel" particularly impactful though, and especially the machine guns feel a bit like pea shooters. Some different sound design for bullet impact, explosions & gun fire would go a long way there. Shooting bad guys is fun though and while they all go down easily it's still satisfying to go for the headshots.

It's not a full 5 stars (yet) because there's room to improve. More branching paths per level would be the main thing I'd like to see added. Maybe the option to take a detour path for extra points/coins at the risk of losing time & taking more damage along the way. Some environmental interaction would also be cool, like shooting down scenery to make it fall on enemies, or even just the usual exploding barrels, that kinda thing. It's cool how currently the enemies can destroy your cover and it would add to the gameplay if you could destroy their cover too.
More levels would be cool, but I'd prefer more depth through better interaction in the current levels.

Overall it's a fun game that's great for its quick "pick up & play"-style timed levels. Not quite a favorite for me yet, but still one I'll keep coming back to.
This game takes me back to the days of pumping quarters into Time Crisis at the local arcade. Looking forward to the new levels and hopefully co-op.

developer_response.body: Count with more levels and co-op :)

reviews.review_description: I was looking for this game.  After Pistol whip this game keeps me busy all day long. Waiting for more missions.

reviews.review_title: Great game but I miss the old voice
reviews.review_description: The game is fun and I enjoy playing it but I miss the old voices like the one from the first game . Is there a way to change it back?
Havent encountered many bugs at all, game is engaging and very smooth, getting shot seems to happen even when you are behind cover but other than that this is a fun game.
Was seriously looking at getting a Time Crisis cabinet, now I don't have to! Thanks for saving my marriage.
This is a great, well implemented idea for vr. Voice acting is a bit spotty but that's to be expected.

reviews.review_title: Did I buy Quest 2 just for this game?
reviews.review_description: Yes and no.

reviews.review_title: Great game! Make sure you have room
reviews.review_description: Awesome game, everytime i play it, i get a little further, but then i miss that little bullet comong from "somewhere". Graphics are a lot better than in part one, weapons too. I would love a little more freedom instead of the "on rails", but even now the game is great. If you are into shooting, get this, seriously.
